responsible_ai_sources.name: AI Governance & Accountability Frameworks
responsible_ai_sources.description: International and national frameworks establishing governance structures, accountability requirements, and oversight mechanisms for AI systems

resources.title: OECD AI Principles & Policy Observatory
Recommendation on the Ethics of Artificial Intelligence
EU AI Act — Governance & Compliance Requirements
AI Risk Management Framework (AI RMF 1.0)
UAE National AI Strategy & Governance
UK AI Regulation: A pro-innovation approach

responsible_ai_sources.name: Bias, Fairness & Non-Discrimination
responsible_ai_sources.description: Standards and guidance for identifying, measuring, and mitigating bias in AI systems to ensure fair and equitable outcomes across all groups

resources.title: Towards a Standard for Identifying and Managing Bias in Artificial Intelligence (NIST SP 1270)
IEEE Ethically Aligned Design — Bias Considerations
Assessment List for Trustworthy AI (ALTAI) — Diversity & Fairness
Understanding artificial intelligence ethics and safety

responsible_ai_sources.name: Explainability & Transparency
responsible_ai_sources.description: Requirements and best practices for making AI decision-making processes understandable, interpretable, and auditable by affected parties and regulators

resources.title: Four Principles of Explainable Artificial Intelligence (NISTIR 8312)
EU AI Act — High-Risk AI Transparency Obligations
Explaining decisions made with AI
Model AI Governance Framework — Explainability

responsible_ai_sources.name: Human Oversight & Control
responsible_ai_sources.description: Mechanisms ensuring meaningful human supervision over AI systems, especially in high-stakes decisions affecting rights, safety, and fundamental freedoms

resources.title: EU AI Act — Human Oversight Requirements (Article 14)
Framework Convention on AI and Human Rights
AI RMF — GOVERN & MANAGE Functions
ISO/IEC 42001 — AI Management System Standard

responsible_ai_sources.name: Accountability & Liability
responsible_ai_sources.description: Legal and regulatory frameworks establishing who is responsible for AI-related harms, including liability rules for AI developers, deployers, and operators

resources.title: AI Liability Directive (Proposal)
Aiming for Truth, Fairness, and Equity in Your Company's Use of AI
Hiroshima AI Process — International Code of Conduct
UAE AI Principles & Ethics Guidelines

compliance_note: Responsible AI obligations vary by jurisdiction and deployment context. High-risk AI systems under the EU AI Act require conformity assessments, technical documentation, and human oversight measures. Consult legal counsel to determine applicable requirements for your AI use cases.
